Author: mithro_
Content: @Leo Moser (mole99) - I'm not sure they do any planarization of the last layers?

Author: mole99
Content: Neither am I, but I'm also not sure that a chip-sized hole in the passivation layer won't cause problems.

Author: 246tnt
Content: That's what nmz said above, PM.4 ?  You need a 2um overlap of TopMetal for any passivation opening.

Author: mole99
Content:
Right. Technically, you could cover the entire chip with TopMetal (no upper density limit?) and open up almost the entire inside area. However, this would be pretty useless since you would then have TopMetal everywhere. Even then, I don't think it would be accepted for manufacture. The rules seem targeted at "normal" bonding applications.

So I guess it's fair to say the min. pad opening is 40x40um, and there's no way around this by opening up the whole chip.

Author: nmz787
Content: Assuming this is "bond pad" rules, then I think each pad would also need to meet "PAD.5 MetalTop (2) overlap of Top_Via (3)" which just says 0.5... not > or <= etc... so I feel they must mean 'exactly 0.5'.
